title: Centrolepis ciliata
intro: Centrolepis ciliata, commonly known as the New Zealand needle sedge or ciliate centrolepis, is a small, aquatic or semi-aquatic plant species in the family Centrolepidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in New Zealand for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Centrolepis ciliata include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Centrolepis ciliata is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create a natural remedy for a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Centrolepis ciliata is a species of flowering plant in the family Centrolepidaceae. This plant belongs to the order Poales and class Liliopsida. It is native to New Zealand and is often found in wetlands and rocky areas. The classification of Centrolepis ciliata is as follows: kingdom Plantae, family Centrolepidaceae, order Poales, class Liliopsida, and genus Centrolepis. Further research may be needed to confirm the specific classification.
